item.title: Memes will keep us together
item.description:
SAY IT! Dr. Vladenfurter
Romance?
.
And why not is this?
The more you know.
.
Rumblie in your tumbly
Calm down, Sheila!
I would wear these clothes.
Let's make them DARKER.
Accurate.
I don't know why this makes you feel better but you do you, boo.
.
Yes please and also now.
.
.
Or now. Now is also good.
.
Heh.
.
.
.
Damn right.
Every time.
My life.
Sit up straight. Spidey commands.
Looking up recipe now.
Truth.
Clippy is everso helpful.
Yup.
.
Thanks Marie Callander.
.
Teeheehee
Accurate update.
Me in a nutshell.
I heart you, imgur.
.
Hyuk.
*grin*
.
You know this pain.
.
.
Old school
You can hear this in his voice.
It's you
I have this and I'm obsessed with it.
Legit.
Cat tax!
item.dc:creator: jennytornado

item.title: Everything is chaos including these memes I have to get off my phone #2
item.description:
My Kitty's name was Mozzarella, but usually I called her Ella.
I think I uploaded some in #1 without sound. Sorry to all 5 people who may have seen that! Also, the unintended theme of this appears to be cats and relationships. I used to have both, but now I just have a cat that my parents are looking after right now because they have a big yard, and I don't.
item.dc:creator: tibby

item.title: This is pretty damn legit actually.
item.dc:creator: JoshMeme

item.title: Surprise Easter kittens!
item.description:
I picked up Pepa and Julietta this morning. They were super scared and hid behind my toilet. No one was expecting them to give birth so soon! Look at the little baby under Julietta! She’s already being a good second mom.
This was a video, but Imgur is acting up. This little guy was cold to to touch, but moving around a lot. It was nerve wracking to move them, but the tile floor was cold.
I tried here first. It was already set up, but she must have been too scared to use it. She kept leaving to hide behind the toilet.
I set up this nursery for the cats with some more cover. I ask Julieta to please use this as her birthing suite. I tried to make behind the toilet an unsuitable location. They’re both in there now.
Clean towel, safe space, and a heating pad. Mama is purring loudly and all of the babies are moving around a lot. I hope she and the babies get a good night of sleep. She came from a loud, crowded environment so I hope she finds peace here. The Imgur community is amazing and has asked how they can help my fosters. We are always in need of supplies! There is a wishlist linked if you’d like to send some kitty things our way. :) https://www.amazon.com/hz/wishlist/ls/1CWKEHIN8OBJP?ref_=wl_share
item.dc:creator: kittycrazy88

item.title: The Television shows that premiered 27 years ago, Wayback in 1995
item.dc:creator: Pasquinel

item.title: The second recovery of Nanna
item.description:
Hi everyone, I'm MrPuckett
This is Nanna, the 16 year old hospice dog I adopted last year Blind, terminal heart disease and a whole list of health problems, she made some incredible progress over the first 8 months at home.
A few weeks ago I posted after Nanna had a stroke. I thought we had our final days together it was absolutely devastating. With a lot of help from Nashville Humane, Nanna made some huge improvements and was almost back to the dog I knew before the stroke.
2 major comebacks for a senior dog that many shelters would've just put down. She was in such a bad state when she was surrender at 15 that even I questioned whether she would ever have a quality life. I figured it was time to update everyone on how she's been doing since the stroke recovery -
EVEN BETTER THAN SHE WAS DOING BEFORE THE STROKE ＼(°o°)／ Here's a video of our first walk together, every painful step, and at the end a clip from our most recent walk
The first mile she ever walked with me took over an hour, a ridiculously slow pace Last weekend she had her longest walk ever as a Puckett!
She's also twice as fast now!!! She's regained most of her balance since the stroke, has been eating and sleeping well and still every month cuddles a little more than the month before.
I started fundraising for the Humane Association with the last post, one of you even donated $2,000 it was amazing. They do so much not only to locate and bring in homeless or mistreated animals, but they've also provided Nanna's extensive medical care every step of the way. It's because of them she got a 2nd and 3rd chance at life, a chance to finally know what it's like to be loved so dearly.
At the end of the fundraiser, 200 people and 100 dogs get together to run a 5k, it's an absolute dream come true it's so fun
Guess who was the fastest dog under 20lbs last year!!!
This year I'm going to take Nanna and shoot for her first ever 5k walk, they even have an award for 'Slowest Dog' It'll be an insane milestone for a hospice dog that could barely walk around the block last summer.
These two just completed their 55th straight Sunday with a 13 mile run or longer They'll sit this one out, but they've gotten so close to Nanna I know they'll be proud of her. The way they bombard her with love when she comes back from a walk is the most wholesome thing in my life.
So we got her all groomed up and ready to train For a dog that's made some incredible progress over the first 8 months, I'm certain that she'll make even more with her time left on this planet.
I can't thank the Humane Association enough for believing this dog had a chance at a great life, and making sure it happened every step of the way. I promised myself I wouldn't get too attached to a dog that doesn't have much time left, and I failed miserably at that promise. She's an important part of our family, and has taught me more about patience and perseverance than I ever thought possible. She's made me a better person.
This is my 16 year old puppy Nanna, she still has the most amazing days ahead of her. .... If you'd like to support the Humane Association in continuing to rescue dogs like Nanna, check out my fundraiser on their page here https://www.classy.org/fundraiser/3793511 ... Thank you for reading my post. I have the day off work and will again be hugging Nanna for every comment below.
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.title: The crazy thing about this, is no one knows where the 200,000 tons of excavated rock went after the people carved it. Not a single trace of it anywhere near. The rock being carved is also incredibly strong, it isn't just soft limestone.

item.title: Russian commanders gave permission to evacuate but relented. Ukrainian convoy turned into a fireball.
item.description: Lypivka, a blip of a village dwarfed by immense wheat fields, was occupied by Russian soldiers for a month until March 31. Here, villagers said the Russians double crossed them. Some village women had begged Russian commanders for permission to evacuate and the Russians seemed to agree. So on March 12th, a group of older men, women and children piled into 14 cars and slowly began to drive to safety. “All of us had white flags and we had permission,” said Valriy Tymchuk, a shopkeeper, who was driving a minibus in the convoy. But then Russian armored personnel carriers swiveled toward them, villagers said. A shell ripped into the first car. And then another. And then another. The convoy turned into a fireball. Mr. Tymchuk said he saw a family of four, including a young child, trapped in their car and engulfed in flames. Many of the singed cars are still on the road. The charred bones of that child are still in back seat, Ms. Tymchuk said. What appeared to be bone pieces were scattered among the blackened metal and heaps of ash. Next to the cars lied two dead pet dogs, fur singed. Mr. Tymchuk barely escaped after his minibus was hit and shrapnel sliced into his face. He shook his head when asked why he thought the Russians did this. “They are zombies,” he said. https://www.instagram.com/p/CcY4o5EAja9/
item.dc:creator: GlobalImageServiceCat

item.dc:creator: MovieCaveDave

item.title: Good people are good
item.description: (Don't try to hard. I don't think I'll change this idea.) --------------------------------------------- Post FP Edit: Thank you for the Accolade awards to whoever decided to grant them to me. :-) Also, I appreciate all you who tried to change my mind by raising some points. Those who just grumbled nonsensical rubbish - long life to you! Finally, thanks to those who helped me cement my world view even more. That's what I assumed would happen. Special thanks to @rumandbass. They reported where Arizona's price stability originates. They have been able to thin the can, double production speed and eliminate advertising costs over the years. Plus - they make less on it (duh). https://www.latimes.com/business/story/2022-04-12/az-iced-tea-inflation-99-cents https://www.foodandwine.com/news/why-arizona-iced-teas-price-stays-at-99-cents So turns out good things and being a good person just takes creativity and work. No magic -- or maybe against apathy and cynical whining, it IS magic. Go figure!! This is how it stays 99c. "The world is a dangerous place to live; not because of the people who are evil, but because of the people who don't do anything about it." -- Albert Einstein Have a good day all. Cheers!
item.dc:creator: epicurusaquinas
